---
title: "Evidence"
subtitle: "The evidence operating layer — from a discovery interview to a decision"
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Evidence <span class="sep">›</span> Overview
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**This section is the project's conscience.** It turns real interviews into structured evidence and
shows the confidence of every claim.
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-neutral no-dot">Real evidence layer · v0.1</span>
<span class="pc-next">[See Claims & Confidence](claims-confidence.qmd)</span>
:::
:::

The evidence operating layer connects fieldwork to decisions. The pipeline is deliberately simple:

::: {.lifecycle}
<li><span class="stage">Discovery interview</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage">Structured evidence</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage">Supports / refutes a claim</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage">Claim supports a decision</span></li>
<li><span class="arrow">→</span><span class="stage is-loop">Decision status moves</span></li>
:::

A customer interview becomes structured evidence; that evidence supports or refutes a **claim**; that
claim supports a **decision**; and the decision shows whether we are still at hypothesis stage or
moving toward a recommendation.

```{python}
#| label: layer-metrics
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
_root = pathlib.Path.cwd()
while _root != _root.parent and not (_root / "src").is_dir():
    _root = _root.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_root))
from IPython.display import HTML
from src.registers import load_decision_register, load_claim_register, load_discovery_evidence
from src.utils import load_raw

decisions = load_decision_register()
claims = load_claim_register()
evidence = load_discovery_evidence()
interviews = load_raw("discovery_interviews.csv")

def metric(label, value, sub=""):
    sub_html = f'<div class="metric-sub">{sub}</div>' if sub else ""
    return (f'<div class="metric"><div class="metric-label">{label}</div>'
            f'<div class="metric-value">{value}</div>{sub_html}</div>')

HTML(
    '<div class="metric-row">'
    + metric("Decisions", len(decisions), "under enquiry")
    + metric("Claims", len(claims), "all hypotheses")
    + metric("Discovery interviews", len(interviews), "real fieldwork logged")
    + metric("Real evidence records", len(evidence), "mapped to claims")
    + "</div>"
)
```

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple" icon=false}
**The real evidence layer is empty — this is correct at v0.1.** No discovery interviews have been
logged yet, so every claim remains a hypothesis. Follow
[*How to Add Real Evidence*](../start-here/how-to-use.qmd) (and the README section of the same name)
to collect the first interview. **No evidence is invented here.**
:::

## In this section

::: {.cil-cards}
::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Confidence</div>
<h3>Claims &amp; Confidence</h3>
<p>Every claim, its declared grade, what evidence would move it, and how much real evidence exists
today — computed from the registers.</p>
<span class="card-go">Inspect claims</span>
[](claims-confidence.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Open Claims and Confidence"}
:::

::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Register</div>
<h3>Evidence Register</h3>
<p>The processed evidence records from discovery interviews — plus how to add the next one.</p>
<span class="card-go">Open register</span>
[](evidence-register.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Open Evidence Register"}
:::

::: {.cil-card}
<div class="card-kicker">Source</div>
<h3>Hypothesis v0.1 · Proposal Source</h3>
<p>Why the proposal is a hypothesis source, and why proposal claims are capped at grade C.</p>
<span class="card-go">Understand the source</span>
[](proposal-hypothesis-v01.qmd){.stretched-link aria-label="Open Hypothesis v0.1"}
:::
:::

## The templates

Collecting real evidence uses two operator templates in `templates/`:

- **Discovery interview** — run and log an interview → one row in `data/raw/discovery_interviews.csv`.
- **Evidence capture** — turn that interview into evidence records → rows in
  `data/processed/discovery_evidence.csv`, each linked to a claim and a decision.
